methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
BurnBot is a Series A climate & energy company founded by Anukool Lakhina and Waleed Haddad. The target operates within a sector comprising 9 private peers (including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen) and 1 public comparable (LNZA), though narrow peer multiples at the Series A stage are insufficient to support peer-median valuation.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable framework. Using total capital raised of $25M as a proxy for post-money valuation at seed-equivalent entry, a 2.0x step-up multiplier to account for progression from Series A to anticipated later-stage positioning yields an implied valuation of $50M. This represents a standard dilution and value-creation profile for frontier-technology companies at this stage.

Given limited comparable transaction data within the immediate peer set, the analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, centered on $50M. The median estimate reflects the single applicable method and the $25M capital base. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular Series A multiples for climate-tech companies of comparable scale and technology maturity, as well as the early-stage revenue or customer-traction data not provided in this dataset. Any valuation refinement would require either narrower peer metrics or disclosed financial performance indicators.
